license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qz6dx9e
title: CGAP Dead cell removal EasySep kit with The Big Easy Magnet
author: Adam Hunter

steps_raw.instruction: A single-cell suspension should have been prepared previously (e.g. by enzymatic dissociation of a tissue) and cells number and viability assessed using 1:1 trypan blue dilution.a.A viability percentage below 70-80% usually justifies using this Dead Cell Removal protocol.
Prepare 8ml of Recommended medium (PBS (8ml) + 2% FBS (160ul) + 1mM CaCl2 (1ul)).

Remove supernatant and resuspend in the appropriate volume of recommended medium (0.25 - 8ml) to obtain a suspension with 1 x 108 cells/ml.a.If total number of cells is below 2.5 x 107, resuspend in the minimum volume, i.e. 0.25ml.
Transfer cell suspension to a 15ml Falcon.
Add Dead Cell Removal (Annexin V) Cocktail to sample:a.50uL per ml of sample.
Add Biotin Selection Cocktail to sample:a.50uL per ml of sample.
Mix (up and down with pipette) and incubate for 3 min at RT.
Vortex RapidSpheres™ for 30 seconds.a.Particles should appear evenly dispersed.
Add RapidSpheres™ to sample and mix:a.100μL per ml of sample.b.No incubation, IMMEDIATELY move to next step.
Add Recommended medium to top up the sample to the indicated volume:a.Top up to 5ml for samples ≤ 2ml.b.Top up to 10ml for samples > 2ml.
Mix by gently pipetting up and down 2 -3 times.
Place the tube (without lid) into the magnet and incubate for 3 mins at RT.

Pick up the magnet, and in one continuous motion invert the magnet and tube, pouring the enriched cell suspension into a new tube.a. Leave the magnet and tube inverted for 2 - 3 seconds, then return upright. Do not shake or blot off any drops that may remain hanging from the mouth of the tube.
Count cells and viability using 1:1 trypan blue dilution.
Add 5ml PBS with 0.04% BSA (200ul) to wash cells.
Resuspend in appropriate volume of 0.04% BSA in PBS to run in Chromium.
